title: Is it not possible to use a negative value in a formula?
excerpt: I’m trying to write a formula with a negative value, specifically -.05 . No matter how I try to write it, I get an illegal formula error.

posts.author: KeeperMonday (Wes Myers)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m trying to write a formula with a negative value, specifically -.05 . No matter how I try to write it, I get an illegal formula error.

posts.author: GCavin (Gilles Cavin - Reinventing Formulas in monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Wes,

You sure can. Please post your syntax.

posts.author: ChristianHa (Christian Harzl)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @KeeperMonday ,

are you using -.05 as value in your formula? Please try it with -0.05 or (-0.05) instead.

Best,
Christian
